name: concise-planning
description: Use when a user asks for a plan for a coding task, to generate a clear, actionable, and atomic checklist.
capabilities: Analyze codebase context including README files and documentation
Generate plans with 6-10 atomic action items in logical order
Define clear scope boundaries with In/Out sections
Include validation and testing steps in every plan
Ask targeted questions only when truly blocking
Provide concrete file and module references in action items
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 4 patterns that are all false positives. The external_commands detections at lines 18 and 37 are markdown formatting (inline code and code fences), not shell execution. The cryptographic algorithm detections at lines 3 and 67 are plain documentation text with no actual crypto operations. This skill is purely a planning template with no executable code, network access, or system interactions.
valueStatement: Turn vague project requests into structured, executable checklists. Reduce back-and-forth by making reasonable assumptions and providing atomic, verb-first action items.
limitations: Cannot execute code or run commands directly
Relies on available context and documentation quality
Does not estimate time or effort for tasks
Planning only - implementation requires separate execution

useCases.title: Feature Development Planning
useCases.description: Break down new feature requests into discrete, implementable steps with clear validation criteria.

useCases.title: Refactoring Projects
useCases.description: Create structured plans for codebase improvements with defined scope and rollback considerations.

useCases.title: Bug Investigation
useCases.description: Generate systematic debugging plans that cover discovery, hypothesis testing, and verification.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Feature Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a plan to add user authentication to our Express API. We use PostgreSQL and JWT. Include discovery, implementation, and testing steps.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starter template for straightforward feature requests

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan a refactoring of our monolithic UserService into separate modules for authentication, profile management, and notifications. Current file is src/services/user.ts (450 lines). Define clear scope boundaries and migration approach.

promptTemplates.title: Bug Investigation Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Users report intermittent 500 errors on checkout. Create an investigation plan covering log analysis, reproduction steps, hypothesis testing, and monitoring setup. Payment provider is Stripe.

promptTemplates.title: Migration Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan our migration from REST to GraphQL for the product catalog API. Include schema design, resolver implementation, gradual rollout strategy, and deprecation timeline for existing endpoints.

bestPractices: Start with context gathering - read README, docs, and relevant code before planning
Keep action items atomic - each should be a single logical unit of work
Always include validation or testing as explicit steps in the plan
antiPatterns: Creating plans without understanding the existing codebase structure
Writing vague steps like 'fix the issue' instead of concrete actions
Skipping validation steps or assuming testing will happen separately

faq.question: When should I use this skill versus asking directly for code?
faq.answer: Use this skill when starting a complex task that benefits from structured thinking. It helps you see the full scope before implementation begins and ensures nothing is overlooked.

faq.question: How many action items should a plan include?
faq.answer: The skill generates 6-10 atomic items by default. This range balances completeness with cognitive load. Larger projects may need multiple plans at different abstraction levels.

faq.question: Will the skill ask me questions before generating a plan?
faq.answer: The skill asks at most 1-2 questions and only when truly blocking. It makes reasonable assumptions for non-critical unknowns to minimize back-and-forth.

faq.question: Can I modify the plan template structure?
faq.answer: The template provides a proven structure (Approach, Scope, Action Items, Validation). You can customize the sections in your prompt if your team has specific planning requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill work for non-coding tasks?
faq.answer: While designed for development work, the structured approach applies to any project needing atomic steps. The verb-first, concrete format translates well to other domains.

faq.question: What makes a good action item?
faq.answer: Good action items start with a verb (Add, Refactor, Verify), name specific files when possible, and represent a single logical unit of work that can be checked off when complete.
